text: The surgeon passes a surgical stapler through the bottom end of the rectal stump, which appears as a rounded structure after the removal of the excess colon.
The surgeon connects the rectal stump to an anvil, which has been inserted into the surgical field.

The surgeon uses a circular stapler to join the proximal colon and rectal stump. As the stapler is tightened, the anvil and stapler head visibly come together, indicating successful apposition of the colon and rectum. The circular knife is then fired, cutting out the tissue and completing the anastomosis.
The head of the circular stapler is visibly flattening as it is pulled out, completing the anastomosis in this laparoscopic rectopexy procedure.
